Tests the Boolean conversion of NaN to ensure it evaluates to false.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Boolean` function when applied to `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The test asserts that converting `NaN` to a boolean results in `false`. This is a narrow functionality script, primarily used to verify the expected behavior of JavaScript's type conversion rules. The assertion will pass if the conversion is correct, otherwise, it will throw an error, indicating a failure in the expected behavior.
